beginner.question: What does HTML stand for?
beginner.explanation: HTML is the standard markup language for creating web pages.

beginner.question: Which tag is used for the largest heading?
beginner.explanation: <h1> defines the most important heading.

beginner.question: Which tag creates a paragraph?
beginner.explanation: defines a paragraph of text.

beginner.question: What does <a> tag create?
beginner.explanation: <a> (anchor) creates hyperlinks to other pages.

beginner.question: How do you insert an image?
beginner.explanation: uses src attribute to specify the image URL.

beginner.question: Which tag creates a line break?
beginner.explanation: inserts a line break without semantic meaning.

beginner.question: Which tag creates a horizontal rule?
beginner.explanation: <hr> creates a thematic break or horizontal line.

beginner.question: What is the correct HTML doctype?
beginner.explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> is the HTML5 doctype declaration.

beginner.question: Which tag creates an unordered list?
beginner.explanation: <ul> creates a bulleted (unordered) list.

beginner.question: Which tag creates an ordered list?
beginner.explanation: <ol> creates a numbered (ordered) list.

intermediate.question: What is a semantic HTML element?
intermediate.explanation: Semantic elements describe their meaning.

intermediate.question: Which element is semantic for navigation?
intermediate.explanation: <nav> defines a navigation section with semantic meaning.

intermediate.question: Which element is semantic for main content?
intermediate.explanation: <main> uniquely identifies the primary content area.

intermediate.question: Which element is semantic for an article?
intermediate.explanation: <article> represents a self-contained composition in a document.

intermediate.question: What does the <picture> element do?
intermediate.explanation: <picture> with <source> elements enables responsive images.

intermediate.question: What does srcset attribute enable?
intermediate.explanation: srcset lets the browser choose the best image for the viewport.

intermediate.question: What does the loading attribute do?
intermediate.explanation: loading="lazy" defers offscreen image loading.

intermediate.question: What does the decoding attribute do?
intermediate.explanation: decoding="async" allows the browser to decode images asynchronously.

intermediate.question: What does crossorigin attribute control?
intermediate.explanation: crossorigin configures CORS behavior for fonts, scripts, images.

intermediate.question: What does integrity attribute do?
intermediate.explanation: integrity hash ensures the resource has not been tampered with.

advanced.question: What is the purpose of <canvas> element?
advanced.explanation: <canvas> provides a pixel-based drawing surface for JS rendering.

advanced.question: What does the defer attribute on <script> do?
advanced.explanation: defer preserves script order and runs after document parsing.

advanced.question: Difference between async and defer?
options: async runs when loaded, defer waits for parsing
async blocks parsing, defer does not
advanced.explanation: async: download + execute ASAP. defer: download while parsing, execute after.

advanced.question: What is the <picture> element for?
advanced.explanation: <picture> with <source media="..."> enables art-directed responsive images.

advanced.question: What does sizes attribute do?
advanced.explanation: sizes="(max-width: 600px) 100vw, 50vw" tells the browser image display size.

advanced.question: What is the purpose of preload?
advanced.explanation: Preload tells the browser to fetch a resource before it is discovered.

advanced.question: What is prefetch?
advanced.explanation: Prefetch hints the browser to fetch resources for the next page.

advanced.question: What is prerender?
advanced.explanation: Prerender renders the entire page in background for instant navigation.

advanced.question: What does the ping attribute on <a> do?
advanced.explanation: ping="url" sends a POST beacon when the link is clicked for analytics.

advanced.question: What does the download attribute do?
advanced.explanation: download="filename.pdf" forces the linked resource to be downloaded.

beginner.question: Which CSS property changes text color?
beginner.explanation: color sets the foreground/text color of an element.

beginner.question: Which CSS property changes background color?
beginner.explanation: background-color sets the background color of an element.

beginner.question: Which CSS property changes font size?
beginner.explanation: font-size controls the size of text, using px, em, rem, etc.

beginner.question: Which CSS property changes font family?
beginner.explanation: font-family specifies the typeface (e.g., Arial, sans-serif).

beginner.question: Which CSS property makes text bold?
beginner.explanation: font-weight: bold makes text bold; numeric values 100-900.

beginner.question: Which CSS property aligns text?
beginner.explanation: text-align: left/center/right/justify controls horizontal alignment.

beginner.question: Which CSS property adds space outside element?
beginner.explanation: margin creates space outside the element border.

beginner.question: Which CSS property adds space inside element?
beginner.explanation: padding creates space between the content and the border.

beginner.question: Which CSS property adds a border?
beginner.explanation: border: width style color (e.g., 1px solid black).

beginner.question: Which CSS property controls element display?
beginner.explanation: display: block/inline/flex/grid/none controls element layout behavior.

intermediate.question: What is CSS specificity?
options: Weight of selectors determining applied styles
intermediate.explanation: Specificity: inline > id > class > element. Higher specificity wins.

intermediate.question: What does !important do?
intermediate.explanation: !important forces a declaration to override any other, breaking normal cascade.

intermediate.question: What is the CSS cascade?
intermediate.explanation: The cascade determines which styles apply based on origin, specificity, order.

intermediate.question: What does inherit keyword do?
intermediate.explanation: inherit explicitly sets a property to the computed value of the parent.

intermediate.question: What does initial keyword do?
intermediate.explanation: initial resets to the property initial value as defined in CSS specs.

intermediate.question: What does unset keyword do?
intermediate.explanation: unset acts as inherit for inherited properties, initial for non-inherited.

intermediate.question: What does revert keyword do?
intermediate.explanation: revert rolls back to the value from the previous cascade origin.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between em and rem?
options: em relative to parent, rem to root
intermediate.explanation: em compounds with nesting; rem always references the root font-size.

intermediate.question: What does 1rem equal by default?
intermediate.explanation: The default root font-size in most browsers is 16px, so 1rem = 16px.

intermediate.question: What does vw unit represent?
intermediate.explanation: 1vw = 1% of the viewport width. 100vw = full viewport width.

advanced.question: What are CSS custom properties?
options: Variables defined with -- prefix, accessed via var()
advanced.explanation: Custom properties enable dynamic style reuse and theming.

advanced.question: How do you define a CSS custom property?
advanced.explanation: Custom properties are defined on elements with -- prefix.

advanced.question: How do you use a custom property?
advanced.explanation: var() function retrieves the value of a custom property.

advanced.question: What is the cascade layers (@layer)?
options: Explicit cascade control with named layers
advanced.explanation: @layer lets authors control the cascade order explicitly.

advanced.question: What is container queries?
advanced.explanation: @container queries elements based on their container size.

advanced.question: What is the scope of container queries?
advanced.explanation: Container query styles only apply within the container element.

advanced.question: What does container-type establish?
options: Creates a containment context for queries
advanced.explanation: container-type: inline-size creates a query container.

advanced.question: What is the has() selector?
advanced.explanation: :has() selects a parent element if it contains certain children.

advanced.question: What does :has() enable?
options: Style parent based on child state
advanced.explanation: :has() finally enables parent selection in CSS without JS.

advanced.question: What is the :where() pseudo-class?
advanced.explanation: :where() groups selectors but contributes zero specificity.
